title: rubus koehleri microgene pygmaeus
intro: Rubus koehleri microgene pygmaeus, also known as the dwarf Korean black raspberry or dwarf Korean blackberry, is a type of flowering plant in the rose family. It is a small shrub native to East Asia, valued for its medicinal and nutritional properties. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and certain cancers. Rubus koehleri microgene pygmaeus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or supplements, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and fever.
